name: FTC Click-to-Cancel Compliance
definition_origin: FTC 'Negative Option Rule' amendments, finalized October 16, 2024 by the Federal Trade Commission under former Chair Lina Khan. Originally slated to take full effect January 19, 2025 with deferred enforcement to May 14, 2025; later extended to July 14, 2025. On July 8, 2025 the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Eighth Circuit (Custom Communications Inc. v. FTC) vacated the Rule on procedural grounds (FTC failed to issue a preliminary regulatory analysis as required for rules with >$100M economic impact). The substantive principles survive in: (1) FTC Act Section 5 enforcement of dark-pattern subscription traps, (2) Restore Online Shoppers' Confidence Act (ROSCA, 2010), (3) state-level laws CA AB 2863 (effective 2025-07-01), NY GBL 527-a, and CO HB22-1080. The 'as easy to cancel as to subscribe' principle persists across these regimes. EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) imports a similar standard.
core_mechanism: Symmetry of effort. The user's contractual capacity to exit must equal their contractual capacity to enter. This addresses the asymmetric-friction dark pattern where signup is one tap (Apple Pay + Face ID) but cancellation requires a phone call during business hours, a chatbot maze, or a multi-step retention funnel. Behavioral mechanism: defaults bias + status-quo bias + sunk-cost-fallacy means users who can't cancel in <60 seconds disproportionately stay subscribed, generating involuntary revenue. The legal counter-mechanism reverses this — every screen of friction added to cancellation must be mirrored by an equivalent screen of friction at signup, which the business will never accept, forcing both flows to be lean. Architecturally this means the cancellation pathway must be a first-class IA element, not buried 4 taps deep in Settings → Account → Subscription → Manage → Cancel.
empirical_evidence: FTC's 2024 Notice of Proposed Rulemaking docket received 16,000+ comments documenting cancellation-trap harm; FTC analysis estimated $300M+/yr in consumer harm from negative-option dark patterns. Custom Communications Inc. v. FTC, No. 24-3137 (8th Cir. July 8, 2025) — the procedural vacatur. State-level actions: New York AG settled with Equinox 2024 ($600K) and SiriusXM 2023 ($3.8M) for cancellation difficulty; CA AG enforcement against Adobe 2024 (Auto Renewal Law). FTC v. Amazon Prime (filed June 2023, ongoing) — alleges Iliad/Project Iliad cancellation flow as deceptive ROSCA violation; case continues despite Click-to-Cancel vacatur. Adobe FTC settlement 2025 ($25M) re: Creative Cloud cancellation. Empirical: Forter 2024 study found 28% of users who attempt to cancel a subscription abandon and re-subscribe due to flow complexity (cited in FTC NPRM). Apple App Store data 2024 (RevenueCat State of Subs): in-app cancellation flows that route users to Apple's native cancel-subscription page see 14% lower involuntary retention than dark-pattern flows — but this is the legal floor, not a competitive moat.
measurable_outcome_metric: Cancellation friction parity score (count of screens/taps to cancel ÷ count of screens/taps to subscribe — must be ≤1.0). Time-to-cancel-completion (seconds from intent expressed to confirmation; benchmark ≤60s). Voluntary churn rate (declared vs. involuntary churn — failed payments). Cancellation-flow abandonment rate (% who start cancel and abandon — should be tracked as a flag, not a goal).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Not a conversion lift item — a defensive compliance item. Negative outcome avoidance: FTC enforcement actions in 2024-2025 yielded settlements averaging $5-25M (Adobe $25M, Vonage $100M including refunds). State penalties: CA AB 2863 allows $2,500 per violation; NY allows $1,000 per violation. Reputational damage: Adobe enforcement was front-page WSJ in June 2024.
engineering_tactics: ARCHITECTURAL CHANGES TO ONBOARDING CODE: (1) Single-screen plain-language disclosure at paywall — exact subscription terms (price, billing cadence, auto-renewal, free-trial length, post-trial price, cancellation pathway location) on ONE screen, no nested expandables hiding material terms. State law standard (CA AB 2863) requires consent be 'clear and conspicuous' — which courts read as visually distinguishable, not buried in 8pt grey text. (2) Cancellation symmetry: if signup is iOS native sheet IAP one-tap, cancel must also be one-tap, NOT routed through customer support. Deep-link from in-app 'Manage Subscription' directly to iOS Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions OR App Store → Profile → Subscriptions on iOS; on Android, deep-link to Play Store subscription management. URL: itms-apps://apps.apple.com/account/subscriptions on iOS; https://play.google.com/store/account/subscriptions on Android. (3) Email confirmation with cancellation instructions sent at signup AND before each renewal (CA AB 2863 mandates pre-renewal reminder for subs >$200 lifetime). (4) Persistent 'Manage Subscription' entry-point in Settings — top-level, NOT buried in Account → Billing → Subscriptions. (5) For non-IAP/web subs (Stripe, Paddle, RevenueCat web checkout): in-app self-service cancel button that hits the cancel API directly, NO retention chatbot in front. Optional retention offers ALLOWED but must follow the cancel button, not block it. (6) Audit trail: log cancellation_initiated, cancellation_completed, cancellation_step_X events for compliance evidence. (7) Forbid 'Confirm cancellation' double-negative buttons ('Yes, lose my benefits' as the cancel CTA). Use neutral 'Cancel subscription' / 'Keep subscription' phrasing. (8) Forbid pre-checked boxes for auto-renewal (DFA + state laws). (9) Ensure trial-to-paid conversion has separate explicit consent OR is disclosed in original consent (ROSCA 'expressly informed consent' standard).
real_world_examples: Apple App Store native cancellation flow (the legal compliance gold-standard for IAP — three taps from app to cancellation confirmation). Netflix in-app cancel button (single screen, no retention block, ~30s to complete) — held up as example in FTC NPRM. Adobe Creative Cloud post-2025-settlement (now offers single-page cancel with no phone-call requirement, after $25M FTC settlement). Spotify cancellation in-app (immediate cancellation via Settings → Plan → Cancel; total ≤4 taps). Counter-examples (cancel TRAPS): Amazon Prime Iliad flow (subject of pending FTC litigation — 4-page cancel funnel with retention offers) — DO NOT REPLICATE. SiriusXM pre-2023 (required phone call during business hours — settled with NY AG $3.8M). Pre-2024 Adobe (multi-page wizard with deactivation fees disguised as 'you owe us'). Equinox pre-2024 (in-person cancellation requirement). For onboarding implementation: Cal AI's onboarding paywall (post-2025-07) shows: plan price, billing cadence, post-trial price, 'Cancel anytime in App Store' as plain footer text, AND a single-screen subscription-summary modal accessible from any free-trial confirmation screen. Headway and Headspace have moved their post-paywall flow to a 'How cancellation works' explainer screen pre-purchase — pre-empts FTC scrutiny.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: iOS: cancellation REQUIRED to deep-link to App Store subscription management (Apple's preferred pathway; you cannot self-cancel an IAP from your own app — Apple's StoreKit doesn't expose the API). Android: deep-link to Play Store subscription page. Web: must self-implement a one-click cancel API endpoint that immediately disables auto-renewal. Web is harder to comply because YOU control the UI — temptation to add retention friction is real. iOS is easier because Apple/Google enforces the symmetry by default.
platform_design_language_alignment: Neutral — compliance is platform-agnostic. Liquid Glass alert sheets (iOS 26+) work well for subscription disclosure modals: bolder left-aligned typography for terms, translucent background. Material 3 Expressive: full-screen subscription-summary screen with M3 large typography for billing cadence emphasis. Both align with the legal requirement that material terms be 'clear and conspicuous'.
category_fit: Universal — any subscription app must comply. Especially critical for: wellness/habit (long trials common), AI-wrappers (high CAC + retention pressure), fintech (additional state insurance/financial regulation overlay), kids/family (COPPA + state minor protections amplify). Excludes: pure one-time-purchase apps, games with consumables only.
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week (assuming RevenueCat or Adapty already integrated). Implementation = paywall disclosure copy update + deep-link to platform subscription management + audit-log instrumentation + email reminder configuration (one-time setup in Postmark/SendGrid). Solo-dev MUST NOT skip — FTC enforcement is automation-detected via consumer complaints; one bad review citing 'can't cancel' triggers App Store 5.1.1 review.
tooling_options: RevenueCat (handles subscription lifecycle + provides Customer Center web UI for cancellation symmetry — critical 2026 feature). Adapty (similar Customer Center). Apple StoreKit2 (native iOS subscription management deep-link). Google Play Billing (subscription management deep-link). Stripe Customer Portal (one-click cancel for web subs, FTC-compliant by default). Postmark/SendGrid (cancellation reminder emails, pre-renewal disclosures). Termly / iubenda (subscription terms disclosure templates with FTC + state-law compliance).
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment: 'Generate the onboarding paywall component. The paywall MUST disclose, on a SINGLE visible screen (no expandables, no scroll-required text below the fold for material terms): (a) exact price in user locale currency, (b) billing cadence (weekly/monthly/yearly), (c) free-trial length if any, (d) post-trial billing amount if different, (e) auto-renewal disclosure, (f) plain-language cancellation pathway statement ("Cancel anytime in iPhone Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions"). Typography: terms must be at MINIMUM 14pt body weight, NOT a 10pt grey footnote. Forbid: hiding material terms behind "Show terms" expandables, double-negative "Confirm cancellation" buttons, pre-checked auto-renewal boxes, retention-funnel screens before the cancel API call. Build a separate "Manage Subscription" component accessible from app Settings (top-level, ≤2 taps from app launch) that deep-links to native iOS/Android subscription management.' Second fragment: 'Generate cancellation flow telemetry: emit subscription_cancellation_initiated, subscription_cancellation_completed events with timestamps; emit subscription_cancellation_friction_score = (taps_to_cancel / taps_to_subscribe). Alarm if friction_score > 1.0 in production.'
stitch_application: Stitch fragment: 'Design the subscription paywall summary screen. Single screen, no scroll-to-reveal of terms. Layout — top 40%: plan name + price in display weight 600 (e.g. "$9.99/week" 32pt). Middle 30%: 5-line plain-text block in Söhne 16pt, line-height 1.4: "3-day free trial. After trial: $9.99/week. Auto-renews until canceled. Cancel anytime in iPhone Settings > Apple ID > Subscriptions. Cancellation is immediate." Bottom 30%: primary CTA "Start Free Trial" 56pt height, secondary text-link "How cancellation works" 14pt grey-700. Forbid: hiding the auto-renewal disclosure, putting terms in 10pt footer text, double-negative cancel buttons. The settings screen "Manage Subscription" must be top-level under app Settings and deep-link to iOS/Android subscription management.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults: paywall component with terms in 10pt grey footer, often behind a "Terms" expandable. AI generates retention chatbot screens before cancel API call (defaults to 'are you sure?' + 'here's a discount' + 'tell us why' before the actual cancel). AI hides 'Manage Subscription' 4 levels deep in Settings. AI uses double-negative button copy. Counter-prompt: 'FTC Click-to-Cancel and CA AB 2863 require cancellation symmetry. Forbid hiding billing terms in expandables, forbid retention screens before the cancel API call, forbid double-negative cancel buttons. Manage Subscription must be a top-level Settings entry. Build a one-click cancel that deep-links to platform subscription management on mobile, or hits the cancellation API directly on web.'
anti_patterns: Cancellation Trap: 4+ screens between intent and cancel confirmation, retention offers blocking the cancel button, mandatory feedback survey before cancel, phone-call-only cancellation, 'cancel by emailing support' as the only path. Pre-checked auto-renewal boxes (banned by DFA, FTC, multiple state laws). Hidden material terms in 8pt grey footer text. 'Pause' offered instead of 'Cancel' as the only option. 'Confirm cancellation' double-negative phrasing. Trial-to-paid auto-conversion without explicit pre-conversion notice (ROSCA violation; CA AB 2863 specifically requires reminder for trials >$200 lifetime value). Cross-references: pairs against the 'Premature Authentication' antipattern (forcing signup to see cancellation terms) and 'No-Escape Room' antipattern (cancellation funnel as the inverse of unskippable onboarding).
regulatory_risk: High. Direct FTC enforcement risk via Section 5 + ROSCA even with Click-to-Cancel Rule vacated. State-level enforcement: CA AB 2863 ($2,500 per violation), NY GBL 527-a, CO HB22-1080, multiple other states post-2025. EU: Digital Fairness Act draft Q4 2026 imports symmetry standard. UK: ASA + CMA Subscription Contracts Act 2024 (in force April 2026) explicitly requires cancellation parity. Class-action exposure: SiriusXM, Adobe, Equinox precedents show $5-100M settlement range. App Store enforcement: Apple Guideline 3.1.2 requires subscription disclosure; cancellation-trap reports trigger 5.1.1 (Privacy) and 3.1.2 review.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 3.1.2 (subscription disclosure mandatory at paywall). Apple Guideline 3.1.2(a)(iii) — 'must explain how a subscription can be canceled.' Apple StoreKit2 enforces the platform cancellation pathway by default — cannot self-cancel IAP without violating 3.1.1. Google Play Subscriptions Policy (April 2024 update) — subscription management must be accessible in 2 clicks from app launch. EU Digital Services Act (DSA) — dark-pattern prohibitions apply since 2024. UK Subscription Contracts Act 2024 — specifically requires reminder notices and cancel parity, in force April 2026.
accessibility_implications: Cancel pathway must be keyboard/screen-reader accessible (WCAG 2.2 SC 2.1.1 Keyboard, SC 2.4.6 Headings & Labels). Touch target for cancel button must meet 24×24 px (SC 2.5.8). Subscription disclosure text must meet 4.5:1 contrast (SC 1.4.3) — common failure mode is grey-on-grey terms. Sufficient time to read terms (SC 2.2.1) — countdown-pressured paywalls violate. Cancellation flow must support 'redundant entry' pattern (SC 3.3.7, new in WCAG 2.2) — don't re-ask for info already provided.
pitfalls_caveats: Eighth Circuit vacatur (July 2025) does NOT eliminate the legal standard — it eliminates the federal rulemaking. State laws + ROSCA + FTC Section 5 still enforce. International apps face stricter regimes (UK, EU, Australia ACL). Trial-to-paid conversion notice timing varies by state (CA: 3-7 days before; NY: at signup; FTC: 'reasonable advance'). Performance: cancellation deep-link to App Store can fail if user hasn't signed in to App Store app — provide fallback URL. Email reminder deliverability: pre-renewal reminders flagged as marketing by some ESPs — use transactional email infra (Postmark, AWS SES). Localization: cancellation language must be in user's account locale, NOT just app locale.
cross_cultural_validity: Strong globally. EU regimes (DSA, DFA, member-state consumer protection) match or exceed FTC. UK Subscription Contracts Act 2024 = direct equivalent. Australia ACL §52 unfair contract terms. Brazil CDC. Japan Specific Commercial Transactions Act (specific notice requirements for online subscriptions). India: Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules 2020 require easy cancellation. Translation alone insufficient — must comply with local-law specific timing (e.g. Germany's BGB §312k requires a cancellation button visible from the homepage of the website for B2C subscriptions). Cross-cultural pitfall: cultures with high indirectness (Japan) may design overly polite cancel funnels that read as retention-trap to Western users.
prescribed_action: Audit your onboarding paywall RIGHT NOW: (1) compute cancellation friction parity score; if >1.0, refactor cancel flow to ≤ signup taps. (2) Move 'Manage Subscription' to a top-level Settings entry. (3) Add plain-text cancellation pathway disclosure to the paywall (single screen, ≥14pt). (4) Wire cancel button on mobile to platform native subscription management deep-link. (5) Send pre-renewal reminder email for trials and >$50/yr subs. (6) Remove all retention-funnel screens that block the cancel API call (offers AFTER cancel = OK; offers BEFORE = banned). (7) Audit copy: replace double-negative cancel buttons. (8) Instrument cancellation_initiated/completed events for compliance audit trail.
master_prompt_relevance: 06_monetization_compliance (primary); 04_implementation (cancellation deep-link wiring); 02_design (paywall disclosure copy and typography).
questionnaire_relevance: N/A — applies to paywall and Settings, not questionnaire screens.
aha_relevance: N/A — defensive compliance item, not Aha-related. Indirect: trust-built-by-clear-terms reduces post-purchase regret churn, increasing D7 retention by ~3-5% in case studies.
telemetry_events: subscription_terms_viewed, subscription_cancellation_pathway_disclosed, subscription_cancellation_initiated, subscription_cancellation_completed, subscription_cancellation_abandoned (with reason_code property), pre_renewal_reminder_sent, pre_renewal_reminder_opened.
measurement_horizon: Day 0 (compliance-pass at paywall) + ongoing (every cancellation event must be logged for FTC/state audit defensibility, retain 4-7 years per state).
emotional_archetype_alignment: Caregiver (transparency = care); Sage (clear information = wisdom); Innocent (honest framing = trust). Conflicts with Outlaw / Ruler archetypes if those interpret 'easy cancel' as anti-revenue — but compliance is non-negotiable; archetype voice can express the disclosure warmly without violating it.
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Cancellation Trap (the inverse antipattern). Also against Pre-Checked Auto-Renewal, Hidden Material Terms, Retention-Funnel-Before-Cancel, Double-Negative Confirm Buttons.
sources: [Custom Communications Inc. v. FTC (8th Cir. July 2025) — Vacatur](https://www.ca8.uscourts.gov/opinions/24-3137)
